ArchitectureNotes.objective: Build a reliable pipeline: video → slide detection → intervals → subtitle alignment → slide images + JSON + Markdown.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: Do not build PPTX export, OCR, LLM descriptions, or web UI in MVP.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: False slide splits due to cursor movement, webcam, or compression artifacts.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: GPU backend may not be available — CPU fallback must always work.

contract.purpose: Define all Pydantic data models used across the pipeline.

param.name: SlidesDocument, SlideSegment, VideoInfo, Roi, SubtitleCue, FrameFeatures

contract.purpose: Load configuration from YAML file and merge with CLI arguments.
Typer CLI with detect, export-md, debug, review commands.
Video decoder backends: OpenCV (CPU), PyAV, Decord, NVDEC. Auto-select best available.
Unified video decoder — select backend, iterate sampled frames.
Crop slide region, apply ignore masks. auto = full for MVP.
Extract pHash, dHash, grayscale, histogram. Compute weighted visual distance.
Compare consecutive frames, detect changes, apply debounce.
Convert change events to slide intervals, choose representative timestamps.
Merge duplicate adjacent segments via hash/SSIM similarity.
Parse SRT/VTT, align cues to slide intervals by timestamp overlap.
Generate debug artifacts: diff_scores.csv, timeline.png, contact_sheet.jpg
Generic LLM client abstraction over OpenAI-compatible API (LM Studio) with vision support and model lifecycle management.

param.name: config
param.type: LlmConfig — provider, base_url, model, context_window, temperature, max_tokens

param.name: LlmClient
param.type: object with chat(), vision(), load_model(), unload_model() methods

contract.purpose: Analyze slide screenshot via LLM vision: extract visual description and on-screen text terms.

param.name: description
param.type: str — structured description of slide content and visible text

contract.purpose: Coordinate full LLM processing pipeline: load model, analyze all slides (vision → description), rephrase transcript with context, unload model, save enriched slides.json.

param.name: enriched_slides.json
param.type: Path — updated slides.json with llm_description and corrected transcript

contract.purpose: Wire all modules together in cli.py detect/export-md/debug commands

DF-001.NAME: DetectPipeline
DF-001.step-1: Load config from YAML + CLI args (M-CONFIG)
DF-001.step-2: Open video, get metadata, iterate sampled frames (M-VIDEO-DECODE via M-BACKENDS)
DF-001.step-3: For each frame: crop ROI, mask ignore regions (M-ROI)
DF-001.step-4: Extract features: pHash, dHash, grayscale, histogram (M-FRAME-FEATURES)
DF-001.step-5: Compare consecutive frames, detect changes with threshold and debounce (M-SLIDE-DETECTOR)
DF-001.step-6: Build slide segments with representative timestamps (M-SEGMENTER)
DF-001.step-8: Parse subtitles and align cues to slide intervals (M-SUBTITLES)
DF-001.step-9: Save representative screenshots and slides.json (M-CLI orchestration)
DF-001.evidence: slides.json exists and validates, slides/*.png files created, debug artifacts present

DF-003.NAME: LlmProcessPipeline
DF-003.TRIGGER: CLI llm-process command or detect --llm flag
DF-003.step-2: Read LlmConfig from config YAML + CLI overrides (M-CONFIG)
DF-003.step-4: Load model via LM Studio API (M-LLM-CLIENT.load_model)
DF-003.step-5: For each slide: read screenshot → vision analyze → structured description (M-SLIDE-ANALYZER)
DF-003.step-6: Save slide descriptions to enriched slides.json, write sidecar JSON per slide (M-LLM-ORCHESTRATOR)
DF-003.step-7: For each slide: description + raw transcript → LLM rephrase with context → corrected transcript (M-NOTES-PROCESSOR with llm mode)
DF-003.step-9: Save enriched slides.json with llm_description and corrected transcript fields (M-LLM-ORCHESTRATOR)
DF-003.evidence: enriched slides.json has llm_description on each slide, transcript corrected with slide context terms, model unloaded

DF-002.NAME: ExportMDPipeline
DF-002.step-2: Generate Marp-formatted Markdown with images and transcript (M-MD-EXPORT)
DF-002.evidence: deck.md opens correctly in Marp-compatible viewer

Phase-1.name: Foundation
Phase-1.goal: Project skeleton, CLI, models, config — verifiable that tool runs and accepts arguments.
Phase-1.step-3: CLI entry point with detect/export-md commands

Phase-2.name: VideoDecoding
Phase-2.goal: Decode video, iterate sampled frames. Verifiable via debug frame export.

Phase-3.name: SlideDetection
Phase-3.goal: Detect slide changes and produce intervals. Verifiable via slides.json and debug artifacts.

Phase-4.name: SubtitlesAndExport
Phase-4.goal: Align subtitles and produce output files. Verifiable via full pipeline with synthetic video.

Phase-5.name: PipelineIntegration
Phase-5.goal: Wire all modules into CLI detect/export-md/debug commands, E2E tests with synthetic video.

Phase-5.name: GPUBackend
Phase-5.goal: PyAV backend with CUDA NVDEC for RTX 4090. Auto-detect + fallback to CPU.
Phase-5.step-15: PyAV backend with CUDA hwaccel support

Phase-6.name: ColabDeployment
Phase-6.goal: Google Colab notebook with T4 GPU — install, mount Drive, run detect + export-pptx, download results.
Phase-6.step-16: Colab notebook: install deps, detect, export pptx/md, Drive upload/download

Phase-7.name: LLMProcessing
Phase-7.goal: Local LLM (LM Studio) vision analysis of slide screenshots + transcript correction using slide context. Verifiable via enriched slides.json with llm_description fields.
Phase-7.step-17: OpenAI-compatible LLM client with vision support, model load/unload lifecycle
Phase-7.step-18: Slide screenshot vision analysis → description + term extraction
Phase-7.step-19: Orchestrate: load model → analyze all slides → rephrase transcript → unload model → save enriched slides.json

DS-COLAB.NAME: GoogleColab
DS-COLAB.purpose: Run the full video-slide-md pipeline on Google Colab with free T4 GPU acceleration.

prerequisites.item: Video file (.mp4) uploaded to Drive or local upload

workflow.step-1: Open notebook in Colab (File → Open notebook → GitHub or Upload)
workflow.step-2: Runtime → Change runtime type → T4 GPU
workflow.step-3: Run all cells — mounts Drive, installs deps, uploads video
workflow.step-5: Run detection → slides.json, slides/, deck.md, deck.pptx
workflow.step-6: Download results from Drive or Colab files panel

limitations.item: Colab session timeout after ~2h inactivity (use Drive mount to persist)
Free T4 has 16GB VRAM — sufficient for 1080p video
Video upload via Colab UI is slow for large files — prefer Drive mount
